page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 30 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 15 physical cores with multithreading enabled, alongside 120 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with hardware support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including VNNI and BF16) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core profile, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core operations, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests, fall within the middle 50% average range. Memory performance is robust, highlighted by a strong Passmark memory score, though raw uncached memory bandwidth write speeds average 25.49 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the instance demonstrates strong text generation speeds on small LLM models, but performance drops to the bottom 25% for prompt processing on larger 70B models due to the lack of hardware acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-standard-30 offers a balanced resource density suitable for mainstream enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of local storage and public IPv4 addresses requires external block storage and networking configurations, which should be factored into deployment planning. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and moderate text processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 30 dedicated vCPUs and 120 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 x86_64 processor. It provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, 15 physical cores, and a cache configuration of 32 MB L3 cache. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database execution speeds. It supports advanced vector and cryptographic instruction sets like AVX-512 and AES. This configuration is optimized for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30 is a general-purpose server with 30 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC processor, delivering strong single-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-30 features 30 vCPUs, 120 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, offering balanced performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server featuring thirty dedicated virtual cores and one hundred twenty gigabytes of memory for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 30 vCPUs and 15 physical cores.
Equipped with 120 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 resource ratio.
Strong single-core CPU and memory performance based on benchmark results.
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic acceleration instructions.
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
Suitable for web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
